Records.description: Three-mass one-loop triangle with all three external invariants nonzero. Finite integral expressed in terms of Clausen functions $\operatorname{Cl}_2$ in the region $p_i^2<0, \Delta_3<0$, and in terms of dilogarithms $\operatorname{Li}_2$ with the variables $x=p_1^2/p_3^2, y=p_2^2/p_3^2$ in the alternative region. Discussed analytically with explicit imaginary parts.
Records.location: Appendix 'Scalar one-loop integrals', Sec. 'Massless scalar one-loop integrals', three-point functions, lines ~70-140

Records.description: Finite 4D triangle with all legs off-shell; result $-1/\sqrt{\lambda} \cdot$ (dilogs $\operatorname{Li}_2$ plus $\log^2$ terms), $\lambda$ the Kallen function of $s_{12},s_{34},s_{56}$.
Records.familyName: massless 1-loop triangle with three off-shell legs

Records.description: One-loop triangle $\operatorname{Tri}(p_1,p_2)$ contributing to the two-loop minimal $n=3$ off-shell form factor of $\operatorname{tr}(\phi_{12}^3)$ in planar $\mathcal{N}=4$ sYM. All three external momenta $p_1,p_2,p_3$ are near their mass shell $p_i^2=-m$ and the operator momentum $q = p_1+p_2+p_3$ is off-shell ($q^2$ fixed to $-1$). The integral is the Davydychev-Ussyukina $\Phi_1$ function evaluated at $x=y=m/s_{12}$; its near-mass-shell expansion through finite terms is given in Eq. (6.1) of the paper and encoded as T1[x] in anc/integrals.nb. Called $\operatorname{Tri}(p_1,p_2)$ or $G_1$ in the paper.
Records.location: Section 3 (Uplift), Eq. (3.4)-(3.8); Fig. fig2loop (a); near mass-shell expansion in Eq. (6.1)

Records.description: One-loop triangle with three massless internal propagators and three off-shell external legs; primary illustration of the diagrammatic coaction in Eq. (1.2). At leading $\varepsilon$ order equals the Bloch-Wigner single-valued dilogarithm in cross-ratios $z,\bar{z}$.

Fully off-shell massless one-loop three-point function used as the pedagogical worked example for expansion by subgraph in the asymptotic Hopf algebra. Leading-$\varepsilon$ result quoted via Zagier's single-valued dilogarithm; expansion in small $p_3$ displayed to first three orders and cross-checked via expansion-by-subgraph (hard-region triangle + soft-region bubble).

Called $T_1$ in Section 4.3 and $T$ in Section 4.1. Massless scalar triangle with three off-shell legs. Propagators given explicitly in Eq. (2): $k^2, (p_2-k)^2, (p_3+k)^2$. Finite in $d=4$, uniform transcendental weight 2. In Example 1, specialized to $p_1^2=p_2^2=-m^2$ with 3-letter alphabet $\{x, 1+x, 1-x\}$, prefactor $P(x)=x/(m^2(1-x^2))$, 17 basis functions. In Example 3, general three-mass kinematics with conformal variables $z \bar{z}=p_2^2/p_1^2, (1-z)(1-\bar{z})=p_3^2/p_1^2$ and 5-letter alphabet. Result: $[2\operatorname{Li}_2(z)-2\operatorname{Li}_2(\bar{z})+\ln(z \bar{z})\ln((1-z)/(1-\bar{z}))]/(z-\bar{z})$.
One-loop massless triangle with three off-shell legs. Leading order is the Bloch-Wigner dilogarithm.

Records.authors: Abreu, Samuel, Britto, Ruth, Duhr, Claude, Gardi, Einan
Records.description: One-loop triangle with massless propagators and three nonzero external masses, finite in D=4. Weight-2 result in z,zbar variables. Single and double cuts computed, integral reconstructed from cuts via dispersion.
One-loop massless triangle in d=2 with generic propagator powers. Result: sv Gauss 2F1.

One-loop massless triangle integral at the symmetric subtraction point p1^2=p2^2=p3^2=-mu^2 with p1+p2+p3=0. Called M^{(1)}_{3,1} in the paper. Three massless propagators D_1=l1^2, D_2=(l1+q3)^2, D_3=(l1-q2)^2. Single master integral; analytic result expanded through O(eps^2) using HypExp/HPL, expressed in terms of Cl_n(pi/3), psi'(1/3), s_n(pi/k), pi powers and zeta values; closed form to all orders in eps available from Davydychev hypergeometric form (Davydychev:1992xr). Used as a building block for two-loop factorized integrals M^{(2)}_{4,2}, M^{(2)}_{4,3} and combined with the bubble M^{(1)}_{2,1} to form the factorized M^{(2)}_{5,1}, M^{(2)}_{6,1} sub-product structures.
Records.location: Section III, master integral M^{(1)}_{3,1} (Eq. defining M^{(1)}_{3,1}); explicit denominators D_1, D_2, D_3 in the denominator list following Eq. for the M-integrals; result expansion Eq. (eq:M131).

Records.description: One-loop three-edge cycle (triangle) graph with all three internal masses vanishing and all three external momenta q_1,q_2,q_3 non-vanishing (off-shell). Theorem 2 of the paper computes the motivic Galois coaction in d=4 dimensions: Delta I_G^m = I_G^m tensor (L^dr)^2 + (a_1 log^m(q_2^2/q_3^2) + a_2 log^m(q_1^2/q_3^2)) tensor (log^dr(f_1) + log^dr(f_2)) L^dr + 1 tensor I_G^dr, where f_1, f_2 are explicit functions of q_1^2, q_2^2, q_3^2 involving the Kallen square root. The result lives at weight 2 (dilog), but motivic logarithms in the coaction cannot be straightforwardly identified with motivic periods of subquotient graphs since the contracted bubble subgraphs are divergent; the paper resolves this via blow-ups / affine motives.
Records.location: Section 1 (Theorem 2); Section 4.2 (triangle with all internal masses vanishing)

Names: massless 1-loop triangle with three off-shell legs

Results.resultTeXPreview: \frac{-2 H\left(\frac{1}{1-z \text{zb}},\frac{1}{1-z}\,;1\right)+2 H\left(\frac{1}{1-z \text{zb}},\frac{1}{1-\text{zb}}\,;1\right)+4 H\left(0,\frac{1}{1-z}\,;1\right)-4 H\left(0,\frac{1}{1-\text{zb}}\,;1\right)-2 \log (z) \log \left(\frac{(z-1) (\text{zb}-1)}{z \text{zb}}\right)+2 \log (\text{zb}) \log \left(\frac{(z-1) (\text{zb}-1)}{z \text{zb}}\right)-\log ^2(z)+\log ^2(\text{zb})}{2 z M_3^2-2 \text{zb} M_3^2}+O\left(\varepsilon^1\right)
